name: Muhammad ibn Muslim
title: Devoted Brother
shortDescription: Ibrahim's younger brother (Tiflan-e-Muslim) who, at the time of martyrdom by Harith, begged to be killed first so he wouldn't see his brother's death.
fullStory:
Muhammad ibn Muslim was Ibrahim's younger brother and the second child of 'Tiflan-e-Muslim'. These two brothers were each other's companion and refuge throughout the captivity and the dark prison of Kufa.

Despite his young age, Muhammad had the bravery and nobility of the Bani Hashem family in his blood. When they were caught by the ruthless Harith by the Euphrates, Muhammad begged him to take them alive to Ibn Ziyad for the reward so he could decide their fate, but Harith was thirsty for their blood.

When Harith drew his sword to kill Ibrahim, the young Muhammad, unable to bear seeing his older brother killed, threw himself over his brother and said: 'O man! Kill me first so I don't see my brother being killed.'

This childish devotion would ache any human heart, but Harith, without an ounce of mercy, unjustly beheaded Muhammad first and then Ibrahim. The heads of these two innocent children were taken to Ibn Ziyad.
quote: (By the Euphrates River:) O man! Kill me first so I do not see the grief of my brother being killed...
